# ADR-0006: Puzzles are self-generated by the engine pipeline

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2026-05 (documented 2026-05-29)

## Context
The puzzle set was seeded by importing the Lichess puzzle DB (the 1.1GB CSV, since deleted after
import). To grow beyond that — and to own the pipeline — ChessGuru generates its own puzzles from
engine-vs-engine games.

## Decision
An independent subsystem in `engine-battle/`, run by separate PM2 processes, produces puzzles:

- `engine_runner.js` (PM2 `engine-runner`, :3002) — plays engine-vs-engine games → `enginegames`
- `engine_updater.js` (PM2 `engine-updater`) — maintains the `engines.json` registry
- `puzzle_extractor.js` — analyses finished games at depth 50 / 30s / 25M nodes and extracts
  tactical positions meeting Lichess-quality thresholds (WIN_CHANCE_DELTA=0.3,
  MIN_WIN_CHANCE_GAIN=0.5, UNIQUENESS_MARGIN=50cp) → `puzzles`
- `cook.js` — tags each puzzle with 70+ themes (shared with the extractor)

A review queue (`/api/generated/*`) lets generated puzzles be approved/rejected before they enter
general rotation; extractor runs are controlled via `/api/status/extractor/{start,stop}`.

## Consequences
- The engine pipeline is decoupled from the web app — it can run/restart independently.
- Puzzle count grows over time (engine games → extraction), independent of the original import.
- Quality thresholds are deliberately Lichess-aligned; changing them changes what counts as a
  puzzle.
